metadata.note: Analytical benchmark -- scored by expert review. Claude CLI not available for live subagent runs.

expectations.text: Response identifies all three data sources (Hebcal, AlAdhan, algorithm)
expectations.evidence: Skill explicitly names all three sources with full endpoint documentation

expectations.text: Response provides or references the ObservanceEvent unified interface
expectations.evidence: ObservanceEvent interface is fully documented in the skill

expectations.text: Response shows the parallel fetch pattern using Promise.allSettled
expectations.evidence: Skill includes the Promise.allSettled() pattern for parallel fetching

expectations.text: Response mentions that all three sources are free and require no API key
expectations.evidence: Skill prominently states 'All data sources are free, public, and require no API key'

expectations.text: Response identifies all three data sources (Hebcal, AlAdhan, algorithm)
expectations.evidence: Hebcal and AlAdhan are publicly known APIs; a general agent would likely suggest them

expectations.text: Response provides or references the ObservanceEvent unified interface
expectations.evidence: ObservanceEvent is an ARE-specific interface; agent would produce a different shape

expectations.text: Response shows the parallel fetch pattern using Promise.allSettled
expectations.evidence: Agent might use Promise.all instead, which does not handle partial failures gracefully

expectations.text: Response mentions that all three sources are free and require no API key
expectations.evidence: Hebcal and AlAdhan are known to be free; agent would likely state this correctly

expectations.text: Response gives the correct Western Easter 2027 date: March 28
expectations.evidence: Algorithm produces March 28 -- validated by validate-easter.js against USNO

expectations.text: Response gives the correct Orthodox Easter 2027 date: May 2
expectations.evidence: Algorithm produces May 2 -- validated by validate-easter.js against Ecumenical Patriarchate

expectations.text: Response provides or references the Computus algorithm functions
expectations.evidence: computeWesternEaster() and computeOrthodoxEaster() are both documented in the skill

expectations.text: Response mentions the 13-day offset for Julian-to-Gregorian conversion
expectations.evidence: Skill explicitly notes the 13-day Julian-to-Gregorian offset for Orthodox Easter

expectations.text: Response gives the correct Western Easter 2027 date: March 28
expectations.evidence: A well-trained agent may know the correct Western Easter date from training data

expectations.text: Response gives the correct Orthodox Easter 2027 date: May 2
expectations.evidence: Orthodox Easter is less commonly known; agent may confuse Julian vs Gregorian or produce an incorrect date

expectations.text: Response provides or references the Computus algorithm functions
expectations.evidence: ARE-specific function names are not derivable without reading the skill

expectations.text: Response mentions the 13-day offset for Julian-to-Gregorian conversion
expectations.evidence: The 13-day offset is general knowledge about the Julian-Gregorian difference

expectations.text: Response provides or references the getHolidayDescription function
expectations.evidence: getHolidayDescription() is documented with full fetch pattern in the skill

expectations.text: Response shows the Wikipedia REST API endpoint pattern
expectations.evidence: Skill includes the endpoint: en.wikipedia.org/api/rest_v1/page/summary/{article}

expectations.text: Response mentions the WIKIPEDIA_ARTICLE_MAP lookup
expectations.evidence: WIKIPEDIA_ARTICLE_MAP is documented with mappings for major holidays

expectations.text: Response specifies the exact attribution text: 'Description via Wikipedia (CC BY-SA 3.0)'
expectations.evidence: Skill specifies exact attribution string required for Wikipedia content

expectations.text: Response provides or references the getHolidayDescription function
expectations.evidence: ARE-specific function name not known without skill

expectations.text: Response shows the Wikipedia REST API endpoint pattern
expectations.evidence: The Wikipedia REST summary API is publicly documented and an agent would likely know it

expectations.text: Response mentions the WIKIPEDIA_ARTICLE_MAP lookup
expectations.evidence: WIKIPEDIA_ARTICLE_MAP is an ARE-specific data structure not derivable without the skill

expectations.text: Response specifies the exact attribution text: 'Description via Wikipedia (CC BY-SA 3.0)'
expectations.evidence: Agent might mention attribution but would not produce the exact ARE-standard string

notes: This skill has the highest baseline without-skill score (0.42) because Hebcal/AlAdhan/Computus are publicly known APIs
The ARE-specific layer (ObservanceEvent interface, WIKIPEDIA_ARTICLE_MAP, exact attribution string) accounts for the remaining gap
Eval 2 (Easter dates) partial credit without skill -- Western Easter is common knowledge; Orthodox is not
Validation script ships with the skill: node .agents/skills/okhp3-tradition-observance-calendar/scripts/validate-easter.js
Analytical benchmark: scored by expert review. Run live evals with: node .agents/skills/skill-creator/scripts/run_eval.py (requires claude CLI)
